---
title: "eda"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
#call packages

library("readr")
library("dplyr")
library("tidyr")
library("stringr")
library("tidytext")
library("ggplot2")
library("forcats")
library("tibble")
library("scales")
library("tidytext")
library("tidyverse")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidytext)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(purrr) 
library(quanteda)
library(quanteda.textstats)
library(stringr)

```

```{r}
all_texts <- list.files("/Users/Ishita/Desktop/text_data/all_texts", full.names = TRUE)
all_texts
```

```{r}
# create document IDs
doc_ids <- all_texts %>%
  basename() %>%
  str_remove("\\.txt$")
```

```{r}
# read in corpus
corpus_tbl <- tibble(
  doc_id = doc_ids,
  file_path = all_texts
) %>%
  mutate(text_raw = map_chr(file_path, read_file))

```

```{r}
# normalize long s (ſ → s)
# lowercase for consistent token matching
# light punctuation cleanup to remove OCR / typographic noise
# No spelling normalization (preserves historical variation)

corpus_tbl <- corpus_tbl %>%
  mutate(
    text = text_raw %>%
      str_replace_all("ſ", "s") %>%
      str_replace_all("[^[:alnum:]\\s]", " ") %>%
      str_to_lower()
  ) %>%
  select(doc_id, text)

```

**Approach 1: TF-IDF lexical distinctness approach**

```{r}
corp <- corpus(corpus_tbl, docid_field = "doc_id", text_field = "text")

toks <- tokens(
  corp,
  remove_punct = TRUE,
  remove_numbers = TRUE,
  remove_symbols = TRUE
) %>%
  tokens_remove(stopwords("en"))

dfm_counts <- dfm(toks)
dfm_tfidf  <- dfm_tfidf(dfm_counts)

# top 10–15 distinctive terms per document (required)
n_top_terms <- 15

tfidf_long <- convert(dfm_tfidf, to = "data.frame") %>%
  pivot_longer(-doc_id, names_to = "term", values_to = "tfidf") %>%
  filter(tfidf > 0) %>%
  group_by(doc_id) %>%
  slice_max(tfidf, n = n_top_terms) %>%
  ungroup()

tfidf_summary <- tfidf_long %>%
  group_by(doc_id) %>%
  summarize(top_terms = paste(term, collapse = ", "), .groups = "drop")

tfidf_summary
```

```{r}
p_tfidf <- tfidf_long |>
  mutate(term = reorder_within(term, tfidf, doc_id)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = tfidf, y = term, fill = doc_id)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ doc_id, scales = "free", ncol = 4) +
  scale_y_reordered() +
  labs(
    title    = "Top TF-IDF Terms by Document",
    x        = "TF-IDF weight",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    strip.text      = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(size = 7),
    panel.spacing   = unit(0.8, "lines")
  )

n_docs <- n_distinct(tfidf_long$doc_id)
plot_height <- ceiling(n_docs / 4) * 2.8

ggsave("tfidf_barchart.png", plot = p_tfidf,
       width = 14, height = plot_height, dpi = 150, limitsize = FALSE)
cat("TF-IDF bar chart saved to tfidf_barchart.png\n")

p_tfidf
```

```{{r}}
write.csv(tfidf_summary, "tfidf_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

**Approach 2: Pearson correlation: similarity and distance between texts**

```{r}
# Pearson correlation approach

dfm_trimmed <- dfm_trim(dfm_counts, min_termfreq = 5)

sim_r <- textstat_simil(
  dfm_trimmed,
  margin = "documents",
  method = "correlation"
)

r_mat <- round(as.matrix(sim_r), 3)

# long-format heatmap data
heat_df <- as.data.frame(r_mat) %>%
  rownames_to_column("doc_i") %>%
  pivot_longer(-doc_i, names_to = "doc_j", values_to = "r")

# remove duplicates + self comparisons
r_pairs <- heat_df %>%
  filter(doc_i < doc_j)

# most / least similar document pairs (required output)
most_similar <- r_pairs %>%
  slice_max(r, n = 2)

least_similar <- r_pairs %>%
  slice_min(r, n = 2)

# print exact numbers
cat("Two most similar pairs:\n")
print(most_similar)
cat("\nTwo least similar pairs:\n")
print(least_similar)

# heatmap visualization
ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = doc_j, y = doc_i, fill = r)) +
  geom_tile() +
  coord_fixed() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "blue",
    mid = "white",
    high = "red",
    midpoint = 0
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Pearson Correlation Between Documents",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = "r"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```

**Approach 3: Syntactic complexity profile**

```{r}
install.packages("udpipe")
```

```{r}
library(udpipe)

udpipe_download_model(language = "english")
ud_model <- udpipe_load_model("english-ewt-ud-2.5-191206.udpipe")

text1_id <- "text 23"
text2_id <- "text 7"

text1 <- corpus_tbl$text[corpus_tbl$doc_id == text1_id]
text2 <- corpus_tbl$text[corpus_tbl$doc_id == text2_id]

ann1 <- as.data.frame(udpipe_annotate(ud_model, x = text1, doc_id = text1_id))
ann2 <- as.data.frame(udpipe_annotate(ud_model, x = text2, doc_id = text2_id))

get_syntactic_measures <- function(ann, doc_label) {
  n_sents <- n_distinct(ann$sentence_id)
  mls <- ann %>% group_by(sentence_id) %>% summarize(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>% pull(n) %>% mean()
  n_clauses <- sum(ann$upos == "VERB", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_dep_clauses <- sum(ann$dep_rel %in% c("ccomp", "xcomp", "advcl", "acl", "relcl"), na.rm = TRUE)
  n_coord <- sum(ann$dep_rel == "cc", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_cn <- sum(ann$dep_rel %in% c("amod", "nmod", "compound"), na.rm = TRUE)
  tibble(
    doc_id           = doc_label,
    MLS              = round(mls, 2),
    C_per_S          = round(n_clauses / n_sents, 2),
    DepCl_per_S      = round(n_dep_clauses / n_sents, 2),
    Coord_per_S      = round(n_coord / n_sents, 2),
    ComplexNom_per_S = round(n_cn / n_sents, 2)
  )
}

syntax_table <- bind_rows(
  get_syntactic_measures(ann1, text1_id),
  get_syntactic_measures(ann2, text2_id)
)

get_example_sentence <- function(ann) {
  ann %>%
    group_by(sentence_id) %>%
    summarize(sentence = first(sentence), n_tokens = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    slice_max(n_tokens, n = 1) %>%
    pull(sentence)
}

cat("Example sentence — text 23:\n")
cat(get_example_sentence(ann1), "\n\n")

cat("Example sentence — text 7:\n")
cat(get_example_sentence(ann2), "\n")

```

```{r}
syntax_table
```
